title: catha dryandri
intro: Catha dryandri, also known as the khat plant, is a flowering plant native to the Horn of Africa and the Arabian Peninsula. It contains cathinone, a stimulant alkaloid that is responsible for its medicinal properties, which include pain relief, fever reduction, and antimalarial activity. The health benefits of Catha dryandri include improved mental alertness, increased energy, and appetite suppression, although excessive consumption can lead to negative side effects such as insomnia and anxiety. Traditional herbal preparations of Catha dryandri involve chewing the leaves or stems, and it can also be brewed into a tea or used in capsule form.
